words.def: the attention that is given to somebody/something by newspapers, television, etc.
words.example: good/bad/adverse publicity

words.def: the profession or business of preparing and printing books, magazines, CD-ROMs, etc. and selling or making them available to the public
words.example: a job in publishing

words.def: a type of loud and aggressive rock music popular in the late 1970s and early 1980s
words.example: a punk band

words.def: a person who is being taught, especially a child in a school
words.example: school pupils

words.def: the act or process of buying something
words.example: to make a purchase (= buy something)

words.def: not mixed with anything else; with nothing added
words.example: pure gold

words.def: only; completely
words.example: I saw the letter purely by chance.

words.def: to do something or try to achieve something over a period of time
words.example: to pursue a goal

words.def: the act of looking for or trying to get something
words.example: pursuit of something, the pursuit of happiness/knowledge/profit

words.def: a game, etc. that you have to think about carefully in order to answer it or do it
words.example: a crossword puzzle

words.def: an exam that you have passed or a course of study that you have successfully completed
words.example: academic/educational/professional/vocational qualifications

words.def: a written list of questions that are answered by a number of people so that information can be collected from the answers
words.example: (British English), to fill in a questionnaire

words.def: to leave your job, school, etc.
words.example: If I don't get more money I'll quit.

words.def: a group of words or a short piece of writing taken from a book, play, speech, etc. and repeated because it is interesting or useful
words.example: a dictionary of quotations

words.def: a group of words or a short piece of writing taken from a book, play, speech, etc. and repeated because it is interesting or useful
words.example: The essay was full of quotes.

words.def: happening or existing between people of different races
words.example: racial hatred/prejudice/tension/violence

words.def: the unfair treatment of people who belong to a different race; violent behaviour towards them
words.example: a victim of racism

words.def: having the belief that some races of people are better than others or having general beliefs about other people based only on their race; showing this through violent or unfair treatment of people of other races
words.example: racist attitudes/remarks

words.def: powerful and very dangerous rays that are sent out from radioactive substances
words.example: high levels/doses of radiation that damage cells

words.def: a wooden or metal bar placed around something as a barrier or to provide support
words.example: She leaned on the ship's rail and gazed out to sea.

words.def: done, chosen, etc. without somebody deciding in advance what is going to happen, or without any regular pattern
words.example: the random killing of innocent people

words.def: a variety of things of a particular type
words.example: range of something, The hotel offers a wide range of facilities.

words.def: the position, especially a high position, that somebody has in a particular organization, society, etc.
words.example: She was not used to mixing with people of high social rank.

words.def: happening quickly or in a short period of time
words.example: rapid change/expansion/growth/development

words.def: very quickly; at a great rate
words.example: to increase/spread/expand rapidly

words.def: a small animal with a long tail, that looks like a large mouse, usually considered a pest (= an animal which is disliked because it destroys food or spreads disease)
words.example: rat poison

words.def: a measurement of the speed at which something happens
words.example: rate (of something), Figures published today show another fall in the rate of inflation.

words.def: a measurement of how good, popular, important, etc. somebody/something is, especially in relation to other people or things
words.example: The poll gave an approval rating of 39 per cent for the president.

words.def: not cooked
words.example: raw meat

words.def: the distance over which you can stretch your arms to touch something; the distance over which a particular object can be used to touch something else
words.example: beyond somebody's reach, The shot was well beyond the reach of the goalkeeper.

words.def: what you do, say or think as a result of something that has happened
words.example: to provoke/cause/get a reaction

words.def: accepting in a sensible way what it is actually possible to do or achieve in a particular situation
words.example: a realistic assessment/approach/view

words.def: fair, practical, and sensible
words.example: it is reasonable to do something, It is reasonable to assume that he knew beforehand that this would happen.

words.def: to a degree that is fairly good but not very good
words.example: The instructions are reasonably straightforward.

words.def: to build or put something together again
words.example: After the earthquake, the people set about rebuilding their homes.

words.def: to remember something
words.example: recall something, She could not recall his name.

words.def: the part of a phone that you hold close to your mouth and ear
words.example: to pick up/lift/put down/replace the receiver

words.def: a difficult time for the economy of a country, when there is less trade and industrial activity than usual and more people are unemployed
words.example: How do you assess the impact of the current recession on manufacturing?

words.def: a set of instructions that tells you how to cook something and the ingredients (= items of food) you need for it
words.example: recipe for something, a recipe for chicken soup

words.def: to think something or have an opinion about something
words.example: I reckon (that) I’m going to get that job.

words.def: the act of remembering who somebody is when you see them, or of identifying what something is
words.example: He glanced briefly towards her but there was no sign of recognition.

words.def: an official suggestion about the best thing to do
words.example: to accept/reject a recommendation

words.def: to get well again after being ill, hurt, etc.
words.example: recover from something, He's still recovering from his operation.

words.def: the process of becoming well again after an illness or injury
words.example: My father has made a full recovery from the operation.

words.def: a person who has recently joined the armed forces or the police
words.example: the training of new recruits

words.def: the act or process of finding new people to join a company, an organization, the armed forces, etc.
words.example: the recruitment of new members

words.def: an act of making something less or smaller; the state of being made less or smaller
words.example: Some staff received wage rises, while others experienced a reduction.

words.def: the official who controls the game in some sports
words.example: He was sent off for arguing with the referee.

words.def: a thing you say or write that mentions somebody/something else; the act of mentioning somebody/something
words.example: reference to somebody/something, She made no reference to her illness but only to her future plans.

words.def: a person who has been forced to leave their country or home, because there is a war or for political, religious or social reasons
words.example: There has been a steady flow of refugees from the war zone.

words.def: attention to or thought and care for somebody/something
words.example: regard for somebody/something, to have scant/little/no regard for somebody/something

words.def: of or relating to a region
words.example: services available at a local and regional level

words.def: an official list or record of names, items, etc.; a book that contains such a list
words.example: The bride and groom signed the register.

words.def: the act of making an official record of something/somebody
words.example: the registration of letters and parcels

words.def: a sad feeling because of something that has happened or something that you have done or not done
words.example: a feeling/pang/twinge of regret

words.def: to control something by means of rules
words.example: The department is responsible for regulating the insurance industry.

words.def: an official rule made by a government or some other authority
words.example: too many rules and regulations

words.def: to make a feeling, an idea, etc. stronger
words.example: The experience reinforced my sense of loss.

words.def: connected with something/somebody in some way
words.example: related to something/somebody, The amount of protein you need is directly related to your lifestyle.

words.def: to a fairly large degree, especially in comparison to something else
words.example: relatively large/small/high/low

words.def: closely connected with the subject you are discussing or the situation you are in
words.example: relevant information/facts/documents/factors

words.def: the feeling of happiness that you have when something unpleasant stops or does not happen
words.example: I felt a huge sense of relief when I heard they were all OK.

words.def: to remove or reduce an unpleasant feeling or pain
words.example: to relieve the symptoms of a cold

words.def: feeling happy because something unpleasant has stopped or has not happened; showing this
words.example: She sounded relieved.
